original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: EAU Guidelines on Sexual and Reproductive Health — Management of Erectile Dysfunction
original_registry_entry.version: EAU Sexual and Reproductive Health Guidelines, 2026 limited update
original_registry_entry.population: Adult men presenting with erectile dysfunction or related sexual-function concerns.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Urology, sexual medicine and relevant endocrine assessment.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for selected medical, sexual, psychosocial, cardiovascular-risk, focused examination and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation. It does not diagnose the cause, recommend treatment or define UAE referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Section 5.4.1 — medical and sexual history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports erectile-function onset, duration, rigidity, morning erections, desire, arousal, ejaculation, orgasm, relationship, treatment and symptom-impact documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5.4.2–5.4.3 — physical examination and laboratory testing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports focused genitourinary, endocrine, vascular and neurological examination, blood pressure, heart rate, BMI or waist, glucose, HbA1c, lipids, morning testosterone and selected hormone context.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5.4.4 and 5.5.5 — cardiovascular and psychosocial assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented cardiovascular, mood, anxiety, life-stressor, relationship and psychosexual context without calculating risk or assigning cause.

title: EAU Guidelines on Sexual and Reproductive Health — Management of Erectile Dysfunction
population: Adult men presenting with erectile dysfunction or related sexual-function concerns.
intended_setting: Urology, sexual medicine and relevant endocrine assessment.
